protocol_definition.name: Sequential Handoff
protocol_definition.visibility: Each agent sees the task and upstream outputs only; no backward messages.
protocol_definition.termination: Stop after one forward pass and Writer output.

final_call.expected_action: Write a self-contained final deliverable using verified upstream work. Recheck every hard constraint and required section; omit meta-commentary and do not add unsupported claims.

intermediate_messages.expected_action: Create a concise execution plan and deliverable structure.
Use the plan and permitted information to collect evidence, constraints, and missing-evidence warnings.
Synthesize upstream work into conclusions tied to the task requirements.

messages.content: You are Planner. Decompose the task into concrete deliverables, hard constraints, factual checks, and a concise execution plan. Do not invent schedules, future work, or external evidence.

You are acting as role: Planner.

Task ID: SE-04

Category: Software Engineering

Difficulty: Medium

Tool Requirement: Prohibited

Available Tools:
[]

Tool Expectations:
{}

Prompt:
Design the backend software architecture for a document-processing and search service.

The service must allow users to upload PDF, TXT, and Markdown files. After upload, it must validate file type and size, store the original file and metadata, extract text, divide text into searchable sections, create a searchable index, support document search, and report processing status.

The design must also support asynchronous processing, retryable failed steps, prevention of unnecessary duplicate records for repeated uploads, easy addition of new document formats, and maintainability for a small development team.

Produce a software design covering: system overview; main components and responsibilities; end-to-end workflow; REST API design; data model; suggested project structure; error handling, retry, and duplicate prevention; testing plan; and at least one meaningful design tradeoff.

Include a Component Table with columns: Component, Main Responsibility, Input, Output. Include an API Table with columns: Method, Endpoint, Purpose, Main Request Data, Main Response Data.

Provide a software design rather than a complete runnable application. Short pseudocode, interface definitions, data structures, example JSON, or code snippets may be included when they clarify the design.

The response should be approximately 700-900 words. No external citations are required. Do not claim that the proposed system has been implemented, empirically tested, or proven to achieve specific performance results.

Required Output Format:
Use the following markdown structure:
1. System Overview
2. Main Components and Responsibilities
3. End-to-End Processing Workflow
4. REST API Design
5. Data Model
6. Suggested Project Structure
7. Error Handling, Retry, and Duplicate Prevention
8. Testing Plan
9. Design Tradeoff

Visible prior context:
No prior agent output is visible.

Instruction:
Create a concise execution plan and deliverable structure.

Return only the content for your role. Do not include hidden chain-of-thought. Use concise reasoning summaries where useful.

You are Researcher. Gather and organize evidence available through the permitted tools and prompt. Reject irrelevant results, preserve exact source identifiers, distinguish verified facts from assumptions, and respect the task tool requirement.

You are acting as role: Researcher.

Design the backend software architecture for a document-processing and search service.

The service must allow users to upload PDF, TXT, and Markdown files. After upload, it must validate file type and size, store the original file and metadata, extract text, divide text into searchable sections, create a searchable index, support document search, and report processing status.

The design must also support asynchronous processing, retryable failed steps, prevention of unnecessary duplicate records for repeated uploads, easy addition of new document formats, and maintainability for a small development team.

final_output:
1. System Overview
The document-processing and search service enables users to upload PDF, TXT, and Markdown files, validates type and size, stores originals and metadata, extracts text, segments content into searchable units, builds and maintains a full-text index, and supports asynchronous processing with retryable steps. It provides document search capabilities and reporting on processing status. The design emphasizes extensibility for new formats, idempotent uploads to prevent duplicates, and maintainability for a small development team.

2. Main Components and Responsibilities

- Upload Service
  - Input: file stream, metadata (userId, filename, size, mime type)
  - Output: upload receipt, temporary processing job id
  - Responsibilities: validate file type and size; store original file; trigger asynchronous processing pipeline; deduplicate uploads when identical content exists.

- Validation & Deduplication
  - Input: file metadata and content hash
  - Output: decision to proceed or reuse existing document record
  - Responsibilities: enforce allowed formats, size limits; compute content hash; check for existing document records to avoid duplicates; emit deduplication result.

- Storage Layer
  - Input: original file, derived artifacts (text, segments, index entries, metadata)
  - Output: persistent storage references (paths/keys or IDs)
  - Responsibilities: store original binary files and metadata; maintain text extraction results, segmentation data, and index shards.

- Text Extraction Engine
  - Input: original file reference
  - Output: extracted plain text and per-section metadata (positions, offsets)
  - Responsibilities: extract text from PDFs, TXT, Markdown (and other formats when added); normalize text; produce structured extraction results.

- Document Segmentation & Indexing
  - Input: extracted text
  - Output: searchable sections with embeddings or inverted index entries
  - Responsibilities: split text into searchable units (e.g., paragraphs, sections); generate or store term vectors / embeddings; create or update index partitions.

- Search Index
  - Input: sections, embeddings/term vectors, metadata
  - Output: search results; ranking data
  - Responsibilities: provide fast full-text search across documents; support keyword and semantic search; handle index re-builds and incremental updates.

- Processing Orchestrator (Worker / Job Queue)
  - Input: job events (upload created, extraction completed, indexing complete)
  - Output: status updates, retry attempts
  - Responsibilities: orchestrate asynchronous workflow; implement retry policies with backoff; ensure idempotent processing steps.

- Status & Reporting Service
  - Input: processing events, user queries
  - Output: processing status, progress updates, result snippets
  - Responsibilities: expose API to query status by job/document; aggregate progress across steps; provide error reporting.

- API Gateway / REST API Layer
  - Input: HTTP requests from clients
  - Output: HTTP responses
  - Responsibilities: authentication (if present), input validation, routing to services, consistent response formats, rate limiting.

- Admin/Observability
  - Input: metrics, logs, traces
  - Output: dashboards, alerts
  - Responsibilities: monitor queue length, success/failure rates, retries; provide health checks; support debugging.

3. End-to-End Processing Workflow

- User uploads file via /upload
  - Validation: ensure mime-type in [application/pdf, text/plain, text/markdown (or text/markdown frontend), application/x-markdown] and size <= limit.
  - Deduplication: compute content hash, check for existing document with same hash and userId; if exists, return existing document reference/status.
  - Persistence: store original file and metadata; enqueue processing job (extract -> segment -> index).

- Processing Orchestrator
  - Step 1: Text Extraction Engine
    - If success: store extracted text and per-section metadata.
    - If failure: retry with backoff; on repeated failure, mark processing as failed and notify.
  - Step 2: Segmentation & Vector/Index Preparation
    - Generate sections, term vectors or embeddings; prepare index entries; persist segment metadata.
  - Step 3: Indexing
    - Update or create index with new document entries; ensure consistency with existing index shards.
  - Step 4: Status Update
    - Mark document as processed; expose status (queued, processing, completed, failed); provide a summary and snippet.

- User Query
  - User sends search query; Search API consults Index; returns ranked results with document references and snippets; optionally fetches original metadata.

- Status Retrieval
  - User or system retrieves status by documentId or jobId via dedicated endpoints.

4. REST API Design

- API Table

Method: POST
Endpoint: /upload
Purpose: Upload a new document and initiate processing
Main Request Data: multipart/form-data including file (PDF/TXT/Markdown), metadata: userId, optional filename override, size
Main Response Data: { documentId, status: "queued" or "exists", processingJobId, message }

Method: GET
Endpoint: /documents/{documentId}
Purpose: Retrieve document metadata and status
Main Request Data: documentId
Main Response Data: { documentId, userId, filename, size, mimeType, status, statusDetails, createdAt, updatedAt }

Method: GET
Endpoint: /documents/{documentId}/content?type=text
Purpose: Retrieve extracted text or segments
Main Request Data: documentId, optional format
Main Response Data: { documentId, sections: [ { id, offset, text } ], fullText }

Method: GET
Endpoint: /search
Purpose: Search across documents
Main Request Data: query string, optional filters (documentId, dateRange, author), pagination (page, pageSize)
Main Response Data: { results: [ { documentId, score, snippet, highlights } ], total }

Method: GET
Endpoint: /health
Purpose: Health check
Main Request Data: none
Main Response Data: { status: "ok" }

6. Data Model

- Document
  - documentId: string (UUID)
  - userId: string
  - filename: string
  - size: integer
  - mimeType: string
  - hash: string (content hash for dedup)
  - originalPath: string
  - createdAt: timestamp
  - updatedAt: timestamp
  - status: enum {queued, extracting, indexing, indexed, failed}
  - statusDetails: string

- TextExtraction
  - documentId: string
  - fullText: string
  - extractedAt: timestamp
  - sourceFormat: string

- Segment
  - segmentId: string
  - documentId: string
  - index: integer
  - text: string
  - startOffset: integer
  - endOffset: integer
  - metadata: JSON

- IndexEntry
  - indexId: string
  - documentId: string
  - segmentId: string
  - terms: [string]
  - vector: array of floats (optional for embeddings)
  - score: float

- Job / ProcessingEvent
  - jobId: string
  - documentId: string
  - step: enum {extraction, segmentation, indexing}
  - status: enum {pending, in_progress, success, failed}
  - retryCount: integer
  - lastUpdated: timestamp
  - details: string

7. Suggested Project Structure

- backend/
  - api/
    - handlers/ (REST endpoints)
    - schemas/ (pydantic/validation schemas)
  - services/
    - upload/ (validation, dedup)
    - extraction/ (PDF/TIX parsing)
    - segmentation/ (sectioning)
    - indexing/ (inverted index / embeddings)
    - search/
  - workers/
    - orchestrator.py (workflow engine)
    - retry_manager.py
  - storage/
    - blob_store/ (originals, extracted text)
    - metadata_store/ (document records, hashes)
  - models/
    - document.py, segment.py, index_entry.py, job.py
  - config/
    - settings.py (env-based config)
  - tests/
    - unit/
    - integration/
  - docs/
    - design.md

- infrastructure/
  - queue/
    - job_queue (e.g., Redis, SQS)
  - database/
    - document_db (PostgreSQL or NoSQL)
  - search/
    - index_store (Elasticsearch or OpenSearch)
  - storage/
    - object_store (S3-compatible)

- scripts/
  - migrations/
  - maintenance/

8. Error Handling, Retry, and Duplicate Prevention

- Validation errors
  - Return 400 with clear message about invalid file type or size.

- Transient failures
  - Processing steps are retried with exponential backoff (e.g., 30s, 2m, 5m) up to a max retryCount (e.g., 5).
  - Failures after max retries mark document as failed and emit an error event to logs/alerts.

- Idempotency and duplicate prevention
  - Compute content hash on upload; if a document with same userId and hash exists, respond with existing documentId and status, skip reprocessing.
  - Use upsert semantics on document records to prevent duplicates on concurrent uploads.

- Concurrency control
  - Ensure only one extraction/segmentation/indexing pipeline runs per documentId at a time; use distributed locks if needed.

- Consistency
  - Use transactional writes where supported (document metadata, text extraction, and index entries) to avoid partial states.

9. Testing Plan

- Unit tests
  - Validate file validation logic for allowed types and sizes.
  - Hashing and deduplication checks; idempotent upload behavior.
  - Text extraction mocks for PDF/TMT/Markdown with expected outputs.

- Integration tests
  - Full end-to-end pipeline with a sample PDF/TXT/Markdown to ensure extraction, segmentation, indexing, and search retrieval work together.
  - Retry logic: simulate transient failures in extraction and verify retries and final status.

- Performance tests
  - Basic load test with bulk small documents to measure ingestion and indexing latency.
  - Query latency under typical search loads; ensure under target SLA.

- Regression tests
  - Ensure adding a new document format does not break existing formats.
  - Verify duplicate prevention across multiple parallel uploads.

- Security & reliability tests
  - Validate input sanitization; ensure no code injection via metadata.
  - Simulated failures to verify graceful degradation.

10. Design Tradeoff

- Tradeoff: Embedding-based semantic search vs. traditional inverted index
  - Rationale: Embeddings improve semantic search quality but increase indexing time and storage. The design supports both: store traditional term-based inverted index for fast exact matches and optionally store embeddings for semantic similarity. This dual approach provides robust search capabilities while keeping implementation complexity contained for a small team. If resources are constrained, the system can initially rely on a robust inverted index and progressively enable embeddings with a separate module and optional feature flag.

agent_effective_config.description: Low-latency OpenAI agent and optional Judge profile.
